concerns.analysis: The extraction pipeline failed on numerous pages (pages 3-11, 22-23, 26, 30, 34-36, 38, 43-44, 46, 48-49, 54), returning empty responses for those pages. As a result, major sections of the document are completely missing from the output, including Sections 1.0 (Introduction) through 9.0 (Criteria for Demonstrating RMP Success).
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
1.0 Introduction
Hart & Hickman, PC (H&H) has prepared this Risk Management Plan (RMP) for the Galaxy Cleaners site on behalf of the North Carolina Dry-Cleaning Solvent Cleanup Act (DSCA) Program...
concerns.output_quote: [Empty response from Gemini for page 3; Section 1.0 is completely absent from the JSON output]

concerns.analysis: The HTML output is missing several critical and meaningful images from the source PDF. Notably, the detailed Survey Plat maps on pages 34 and 43 of the source document are entirely missing from the rendered HTML under their respective 'REDUCTION OF SURVEY PLAT' headings. Additionally, the line graphs on pages 18 and 19 have been replaced with text descriptions enclosed in brackets (e.g., '[Image: A line chart displaying...]') rather than actual elements, depriving the user of the visual da
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 34 contains a detailed map titled 'SURVEY PLAT - EXHIBIT A'

concerns.description:
Header/footer elements retained in output (14): 2923 South Tryon Street, Suite 100
Charlotte, NC 28203
704.5; 3334 Hillsborough Street
Raleigh, NC 27607
919.847.4241 main; i
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=14, pages_affected=11/54. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML across most pages — pervasive pipeline failure. Screen readers will encounter duplicate artifacts on every page.

concerns.description: Significant word count loss: 42% of baseline
Table split across page break(s): 1 table(s) with repeated headers across consecutive pages
concerns.analysis: split_table_count=1. Examples: pages 16-17: 2 cols (table 8: analytical data for groundwater, adt 8...). Tables spanning multiple pages were extracted as separate table elements with duplicated header rows. The HTML output will show multiple tables instead of one continuous table — a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: Table caption coded as header row: 2 table(s) have a single spanning first row that is likely a caption/title
concerns.analysis:
caption_as_header=2. Examples: p14: "TITLE
GROUNDWATER CONTAMINANT CONCENTRATION MAP"; p15: "TITLE
SOIL CONTAMINANT
CONCENTRATION MAP". First row spans all columns — should be a <caption> element, not a header row.

concerns.description: Full-page image alongside extracted text (1 page(s))
concerns.analysis: suspect_pages=1. A scanned/screenshot page image duplicates content already present as text elements.

concerns.description: 1 bare URL(s) in text not converted to hyperlinks: www.ncdsca.org,
concerns.analysis: bare_url_count=1. URLs appear as plain text instead of clickable links. Per NCDIT criteria, link text not wrapped in href is a major defect.

concerns.description: Mixed numbering types at same list level (1 list(s)): p28:letter+roman — possible sub-point flattening
concerns.analysis: flat_lists=1. Lists with mixed numbering types (numeric+letter or numeric+roman) at the same depth level suggest sub-points were flattened.

concerns.description: Form elements detected: 5 form(s) with 11 field(s) — forms lose functionality in HTML
concerns.analysis: form_count=5, total_fields=11. PDF form fields (checkboxes, text inputs, signatures) cannot be faithfully rendered as static HTML.

concerns.description: Repeated footer/header text (2x): "if you have any questions, please feel free to contact me at"
concerns.analysis: Short text with footer-like pattern appears on 2 pages

concerns.description: Repeated footer/header text (2x): "cc: dsca site # dc920029 file"
concerns.analysis: Short text with footer-like pattern appears on 2 pages

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 1 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=1, examples=['https://www.harthickman.com']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 2 table(s) with 3+ rows have no <th> header cells
concerns.analysis: data_in_th_time=0, no_th_tables=2. Minor semantic markup issues: tables lack <th> elements or use time values as headers. These affect screen reader context but are less severe than data misrepresentation.
